target: 100% yacc grammar parity with recursive descent parser

batches.name: infrastructure
batches.description: Parser struct, advance/peek/match/expect/pos/mapTokenType helpers

batches.name: names
batches.description: Identifiers, qualified names, name lists

batches.name: types
batches.description: Type names, array bounds, type modifiers

batches.name: expressions
batches.description: All expression types: a_expr, b_expr, c_expr, operators, subqueries

batches.name: functions
batches.description: Function calls, window functions, aggregates

batches.name: xml
batches.description: XML expressions and functions

batches.name: json
batches.description: JSON expressions, constructors, queries

batches.name: select
batches.description: SELECT statement and all clauses

batches.name: insert
batches.description: INSERT statement

batches.name: update
batches.description: UPDATE, DELETE, MERGE statements

batches.name: create_table
batches.description: CREATE TABLE, column definitions, constraints

batches.name: alter_table
batches.description: ALTER TABLE and all subcommands

batches.name: create_index
batches.description: CREATE INDEX

batches.name: create_view
batches.description: CREATE VIEW, CREATE TABLE AS

batches.name: create_function
batches.description: CREATE FUNCTION/PROCEDURE

batches.name: alter_misc
batches.description: ALTER FUNCTION, ALTER TYPE, ALTER SEQUENCE, ALTER generic

batches.name: drop
batches.description: DROP, TRUNCATE

batches.name: sequence
batches.description: CREATE/ALTER SEQUENCE

batches.name: grant
batches.description: GRANT/REVOKE, policies

batches.name: schema
batches.description: CREATE SCHEMA, RENAME, COMMENT

batches.name: define
batches.description: DefineStmt, aggregate, operator, opclass definitions

batches.name: trigger
batches.description: CREATE TRIGGER, event triggers

batches.name: copy
batches.description: COPY statement

batches.name: set
batches.description: SET, SHOW, RESET, variable handling

batches.name: transaction
batches.description: Transaction statements

batches.name: cursor
batches.description: DECLARE CURSOR, FETCH, MOVE

batches.name: prepare
batches.description: PREPARE, EXECUTE, DEALLOCATE

batches.name: maintenance
batches.description: VACUUM, ANALYZE, CLUSTER, REINDEX

batches.name: lock
batches.description: LOCK, CONSTRAINTS SET

batches.name: utility
batches.description: Utility options, DO, EXPLAIN, LISTEN, NOTIFY, etc.

batches.name: database
batches.description: CREATE/ALTER DATABASE

batches.name: fdw
batches.description: Foreign data wrappers, foreign servers, foreign tables

batches.name: extension
batches.description: CREATE/ALTER EXTENSION, ACCESS METHOD, CAST, TRANSFORM

batches.name: publication
batches.description: Publication, subscription, rule

batches.name: stmt_dispatch
batches.description: Top-level stmtblock/stmtmulti/stmt dispatching, wire up Parse() entry point

batches.name: call_stmt
batches.description: CALL statement for invoking procedures. Add CALL case to parseStmt dispatch. Ref: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/sql-call.html

batches.name: alter_system
batches.description: ALTER SYSTEM SET/RESET for modifying postgresql.conf. Add ALTER SYSTEM case to parseStmt ALTER dispatch. Ref: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/sql-altersystem.html

batches.name: tablespace
batches.description: CREATE TABLESPACE, DROP TABLESPACE, and full ALTER TABLESPACE (SET/RESET reloptions). Currently ALTER TABLESPACE only handles OWNER/RENAME, missing SET/RESET. Refs: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/sql-createtablespace.html, https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/sql-droptablespace.html, https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/sql-altertablespace.html

batches.name: create_conversion
batches.description: CREATE [DEFAULT] CONVERSION statement. Add CONVERSION case to parseCreateDispatch. Ref: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/sql-createconversion.html

batches.name: drop_operator
batches.description: DROP OPERATOR with oper_argtypes (RemoveOperStmt). Currently has a TODO placeholder returning nil. Must parse operator_with_argtypes_list. Ref: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/sql-dropoperator.html

batches.name: drop_func_argtypes
batches.description: Fix DROP FUNCTION/PROCEDURE/ROUTINE/AGGREGATE to use function_with_argtypes_list instead of any_name_list. The yacc grammar (RemoveFuncStmt, RemoveAggrStmt) parses argument types like DROP FUNCTION foo(integer, text). Current parseDropAnyNameList only parses names without arg types. Refs: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/sql-dropfunction.html, https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/sql-dropprocedure.html, https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/sql-dropaggregate.html

batches.name: alter_tablespace_set_reset
batches.description: Complete ALTER TABLESPACE SET/RESET reloptions (AlterTableSpaceOptionsStmt). Currently parseAlterTablespaceOwner handles OWNER/RENAME but returns nil for SET. Must produce AlterTableSpaceOptionsStmt with IsReset flag. Ref: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/sql-altertablespace.html

batches.name: func_table_in_from
batches.description: Fix batch 7 gap: parseRelationOrFuncTable does not handle bare function calls (func_table) in FROM clause. Also parseTableRefPrimary does not dispatch XMLTABLE/JSON_TABLE tokens, and parseLateralTableRef lacks LATERAL XMLTABLE/JSON_TABLE. Must disambiguate qualified_name followed by '(' as func_expr_windowless, and add XMLTABLE/JSON_TABLE cases to parseTableRefPrimary and parseLateralTableRef. Refs: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/sql-select.html, https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/functions-xml.html#FUNCTIONS-XML-PROCESSING-XMLTABLE, https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/functions-json.html#FUNCTIONS-SQLJSON-TABLE

batches.name: cte_search_cycle
batches.description: Fix batch 7 gap: parseCommonTableExpr skips opt_search_clause and opt_cycle_clause (lines 561-562 comment 'rarely used, skip for now'). Must implement SEARCH BREADTH/DEPTH FIRST BY column_list SET column and CYCLE column_list SET column TO value DEFAULT value USING column. Ref: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/queries-with.html

batches.name: begin_atomic_routine_body
batches.description: Fix batch 14 gap: parseOptRoutineBody has TODO stub at line 525 for BEGIN ATOMIC routine_body_stmt_list END. Must parse BEGIN_P ATOMIC, then loop calling parseStmt for each statement (separated by ';'), then expect END_P. Ref: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/sql-createfunction.html

batches.name: func_set_reset_options
batches.description: Fix batch 14 gap: parseCommonFuncOptItem BNF lists SET set_rest_more and VariableResetStmt (lines 442-443) but the switch has no cases for them. Must add SET case that calls parseSetRestMore and RESET case that calls parseVariableResetStmt, wrapping results in DefElem. Ref: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/sql-createfunction.html

batches.name: alter_rule
batches.description: New: ALTER RULE name ON table_name RENAME TO new_name. Width gap — ALTER RULE is not dispatched in parseStmt ALTER switch. Add case RULE to ALTER dispatch in parser.go, implement parseAlterRuleStmt producing RenameStmt with OBJECT_RULE. Ref: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/sql-alterrule.html

batches.name: alter_relation_set_schema
batches.description: Fix batch 11 gap: parseAlterTable, parseAlterView, parseAlterIndex, parseAlterMaterializedView, parseAlterForeignTable do not handle SET SCHEMA. In PG yacc grammar, ALTER TABLE/VIEW/INDEX/MATVIEW/FOREIGN TABLE ... SET SCHEMA produces AlterObjectSchemaStmt, but parseAlterTableSet has no SCHEMA case and the separate parseAlter* functions don't check for it before delegating to parseAlterTableCmds. Must add SET SCHEMA dispatch before parseAlterTableCmds in each function. Ref: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/sql-altertable.html

batches.name: alter_sequence_owner_schema
batches.description: Fix batch 11 gap: parseAlterSequence only handles RENAME TO and sequence options via parseSeqOptList. Missing OWNER TO (AlterOwnerStmt) and SET SCHEMA (AlterObjectSchemaStmt). Must add OWNER and SET SCHEMA checks before falling through to parseSeqOptList. Ref: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/sql-altersequence.html

batches.name: alter_database_rename_owner_refresh
batches.description: Fix batch 30 gap: parseAlterDatabaseDispatch does not handle RENAME TO (RenameStmt), OWNER TO (AlterOwnerStmt), or REFRESH COLLATION VERSION (AlterDatabaseRefreshCollStmt). RENAME falls to default which tries createdb_opt_list and fails. OWNER is parsed as a createdb option producing wrong AST. Must add explicit cases for RENAME, OWNER, and REFRESH. Ref: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/sql-alterdatabase.html

batches.name: createdb_pg17_options
batches.description: Fix batch 30 gap: parseCreatedbOptName only recognizes CONNECTION LIMIT, ENCODING, LOCATION, OWNER, TABLESPACE, TEMPLATE, and IDENT. PG 17 added many options as reserved/unreserved keywords: LOCALE, LC_COLLATE, LC_CTYPE, ICU_LOCALE, ICU_RULES, LOCALE_PROVIDER, COLLATION_VERSION, ALLOW_CONNECTIONS, IS_TEMPLATE, OID, STRATEGY, BUILTIN_LOCALE. These keywords won't match IDENT and silently fail. Must add explicit cases. Ref: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/sql-createdatabase.html

batches.name: alter_index_matview_depends_ext
batches.description: Fix batch 11 gap: parseAlterIndex and parseAlterMaterializedView do not handle [NO] DEPENDS ON EXTENSION (AlterObjectDependsStmt). In PG grammar, ALTER INDEX/MATERIALIZED VIEW name [NO] DEPENDS ON EXTENSION ext_name produces AlterObjectDependsStmt. Must add DEPENDS and NO DEPENDS checks before falling through to parseAlterTableCmds. Ref: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/sql-alterindex.html

batches.name: alter_trigger_rename
batches.description: Fix batch 21 gap: parseAlterTriggerDependsOnExtension only handles [NO] DEPENDS ON EXTENSION variant. Missing ALTER TRIGGER name ON table_name RENAME TO new_name which produces RenameStmt with OBJECT_TRIGGER. Must dispatch on RENAME vs DEPENDS/NO after parsing trigger name and ON relation. Ref: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/sql-altertrigger.html

batches.name: alter_extension_set_schema
batches.description: Fix batch 32 gap: parseAlterExtensionStmt only handles UPDATE, ADD, DROP. Missing ALTER EXTENSION name SET SCHEMA new_schema which produces AlterObjectSchemaStmt with OBJECT_EXTENSION. Must add SET case checking for SCHEMA keyword. Ref: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/sql-alterextension.html

batches.name: alter_server_owner_rename
batches.description: Fix batch 31 gap: parseAlterForeignServerStmt only handles VERSION and OPTIONS. Missing OWNER TO (AlterOwnerStmt with OBJECT_FOREIGN_SERVER) and RENAME TO (RenameStmt with OBJECT_FOREIGN_SERVER). Must add OWNER and RENAME cases before VERSION/OPTIONS dispatch. Ref: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/sql-alterserver.html

batches.name: alter_role_group_rename
batches.description: Fix batch 18 gap: parseAlterRoleStmt missing RENAME TO (RenameStmt with OBJECT_ROLE). parseAlterGroupStmt missing RENAME TO (RenameStmt with OBJECT_ROLE). Must add RENAME case to both functions. ALTER USER RENAME TO is covered by parseAlterRoleStmt since ALTER USER dispatches there. Refs: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/sql-alterrole.html, https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/sql-altergroup.html

batches.name: alter_publication_owner_rename
batches.description: Fix batch 33 gap: parseAlterPublicationStmt only handles SET/ADD/DROP. Missing OWNER TO (AlterOwnerStmt with OBJECT_PUBLICATION) and RENAME TO (RenameStmt with OBJECT_PUBLICATION). Must add OWNER and RENAME cases to the switch. Ref: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/sql-alterpublication.html

batches.name: alter_subscription_owner_rename
batches.description: Fix batch 33 gap: parseAlterSubscriptionStmt only handles SET/ADD/DROP/CONNECTION/REFRESH/ENABLE/DISABLE/SKIP. Missing OWNER TO (AlterOwnerStmt with OBJECT_SUBSCRIPTION) and RENAME TO (RenameStmt with OBJECT_SUBSCRIPTION). Must add OWNER and RENAME cases to the switch. Ref: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/sql-altersubscription.html

batches.name: is_normalized_expr
batches.description: Fix batch 3 gap: parseIsPostfix has a deferred stub for IS [NOT] [NFC|NFD|NFKC|NFKD] NORMALIZED that just returns left operand unchanged. Must produce a proper FuncCall to unicode_is_normalized(expr, form) or equivalent AST node. The unicode_normal_form rule is already implemented. Ref: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/functions-string.html

batches.name: alter_fdw_owner_rename
batches.description: Fix batch 31 gap: parseAlterFdwStmt only handles HANDLER/VALIDATOR/OPTIONS. Missing OWNER TO (AlterOwnerStmt with OBJECT_FDW) and RENAME TO (RenameStmt with OBJECT_FDW). Must add OWNER and RENAME cases before falling through to FDW options parsing, similar to pattern in parseAlterForeignServerStmt. Ref: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/sql-alterforeigndatawrapper.html

batches.name: alter_statistics_rename_owner_schema
batches.description: Fix batch 20 gap: parseAlterStatisticsStmt only handles SET STATISTICS value. Missing RENAME TO (RenameStmt with OBJECT_STATISTIC_EXT), OWNER TO (AlterOwnerStmt with OBJECT_STATISTIC_EXT), and SET SCHEMA (AlterObjectSchemaStmt with OBJECT_STATISTIC_EXT). Must dispatch on next token after name before expecting SET STATISTICS. Ref: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/sql-alterstatistics.html

batches.name: set_catalog_stmt
batches.description: Fix batch 23 gap: SET CATALOG Sconst returns nil instead of VariableSetStmt. Should construct VariableSetStmt with kind=VAR_SET_VALUE, name="catalog"

batches.name: ext_oper_argtypes_unary
batches.description: Fix batch 32 gap: parseExtOperArgtypes returns nil for single-argument operators (Typename) instead of list_make1(typ). Should return &nodes.List{Items: []nodes.Node{typ1}}
